concept_name: Why a Magnetic Force Can't Speed You Up — F ⊥ v Means No Work
source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism — table-of-contents reference only (the 'force on a moving charge' heading; the 'does no work' remark that sits between the magnitude lesson and circular motion); teaching authored from first principles. NCERT consulted for the Indian real-world anchor only. This concept is the NO-WORK / energy sibling of magnetic_force_moving_charge: it teaches WHY the magnetic force can never change a charge's speed — because F = q(v × B) is perpendicular to v at every instant, the work it does is W = F·d·cos90° = 0, so by the work-energy theorem the kinetic energy (and therefore the speed) is locked; the force only TURNS the velocity, it never adds energy. It deliberately does NOT teach the magnitude F = qvB sin(theta), the circular-motion radius r = mv/qB, the period T, or any Newton/Tesla number — those belong to magnetic_force_moving_charge and are NEVER shown on-canvas here. No force-magnitude readout appears anywhere (hide_magnitude_readout on every state); the F arrow is direction-only. Conceptual-only ship (no mode_overrides). EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + predict→reveal). Renders on field_3d via the magnetic_no_work scenario (the no-work sibling of lorentz_force_uniform_field), which adds the |v| speed meter, the W work meter, the equal-arc trail, and the STATE_5 electric-vs-magnetic split screen. Lighter atom by founder carve-out: no assessment block, no coverage_map.

real_world_anchor.primary: At the VECC accelerator in Kolkata, charged particles are whipped round and round a giant ring. Huge magnets sit at every bend, and their whole job is to STEER the beam — to bend it round each corner and keep it on the track. But the magnets never make the beam any faster. Speeding the beam up is a completely different machine's job. A magnetic force is a steering wheel, not an accelerator pedal: it can turn you, but it can never push you faster.
real_world_anchor.secondary: In a cloud chamber at a lab like TIFR in Mumbai, a charged particle leaves a curving track as it crosses a magnetic field. The track curves — the direction keeps changing — but the spacing of the trail stays even all the way round, because the speed never changes. The magnetic force bent its path without ever speeding it up.

v.name: Speed of the moving charge (the |v| meter shows this; it stays locked on the magnetic side)

B.name: Magnetic field strength (bigger B = a TIGHTER turn at the SAME speed, never faster)

q_sign.name: Sign of the charge: +1 positive, -1 negative

E.name: Electric field strength on the LEFT (electric) side of the STATE_5 split screen — controls how fast that charge speeds up

formulas.force_is_perpendicular_to_velocity: The magnetic force is always perpendicular to the velocity — F · v = 0 at every instant
formulas.perpendicular_force_does_no_work: Work is force times displacement times cos of the angle between them; the angle is 90°, cos 90° is zero, so the work done is exactly zero
formulas.work_energy_theorem: Zero work means zero change in kinetic energy — so the speed cannot change; the magnetic force only turns the velocity
formulas.speed_is_locked_direction_turns: The magnetic force changes the DIRECTION of the velocity but never its size; the speed stays constant while the path curves
formulas.electric_force_can_do_work: An electric force is NOT perpendicular to the motion, so it has a component along v, does work, and speeds the charge up — the opposite of the magnetic case
formulas.stronger_field_tighter_turn: A stronger magnetic field makes a TIGHTER turn at the SAME speed — it steers harder, it never accelerates

constraints: This concept teaches that the magnetic force does NO WORK and so cannot change a charge's speed — it shows NO magnitude number, NO F = qvB sin(theta), NO radius r = mv/qB, NO period T, and NO Newton or Tesla value anywhere on the canvas
The magnetic force is perpendicular to the velocity at every instant, so F · v = 0 always (the magnetic force does zero work)
On the magnetic side the speed |v| is constant — the work-energy theorem gives ΔKE = 0 because the work done is zero
Equal time means equal arc length on the magnetic side; the evenly spaced trail IS the visual proof that the speed is constant
The F arrow is direction-only — it shows which way the force points, never how strong it is (no magnitude readout)
The |v| speed meter reads the speed (KE = ½mv²), never a force; the W work meter reads W = ∫ F·v dt, which is zero on the magnetic side and climbing on the electric side
On the STATE_5 split screen both charges START at the same speed; the electric force does work (speed rises, parabolic path) while the magnetic force does none (speed flat, curving path)
When v is parallel to B there is no magnetic force at all and the path is a straight line
The radius, period, and force magnitude are deliberately NOT shown here — they belong to magnetic_force_moving_charge

aha_moment.statement: A magnetic force steers you — it turns the velocity but never adds speed.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Two identical charges start at the same speed: the electric one's speed meter climbs and its path is a parabola, while the magnetic one's speed meter stays flat with the work meter pinned at zero as its path curves.

STATE_1.title: Round and Round — so it must be speeding up?

scene_composition.text: a charge drifts in — then we switch on a magnetic field

its path curls into
a closed loop
round and round... so it must be speeding up — right?

tts_sentences.text_en: A charged particle drifts in, and we switch on a magnetic field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक charged particle अंदर drift करता है, और हम magnetic field switch on करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక charged particle లోపలికి drift అవుతుంది, మరియు మనం magnetic field switch on చేస్తాం।

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch — its path curls round and round into a closed loop.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखो — इसका path round and round curl होकर एक closed loop बन जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి — దాని path round and round curl అయి ఒక closed loop గా మారుతుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: It's going round and round... so it must be speeding up — right?
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह round and round जा रहा है... तो यह ज़रूर speed up हो रहा होगा — है ना?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది round and round వెళ్తోంది... అంటే ఇది speed up అవుతూ ఉండాలి — కదా?

STATE_2.title: Square Across — F is perpendicular to v

scene_composition.text: Predict: which way does the force point as it orbits?

green F sits at a RIGHT
ANGLE to orange v —
always toward the centre
you found this with the right-hand rule last lesson

tts_sentences.text_en: Before I show the force — which way does the magnetic force point as it orbits?
tts_sentences.text_hi: force दिखाने से पहले — जब यह orbit करता है, तो magnetic force किस direction में point करता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: force చూపించే ముందు — ఇది orbit చేస్తున్నప్పుడు, magnetic force ఏ direction లో point చేస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: Here it is: the force is green, and it sits at a right angle to the orange velocity.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए: force हरे रंग का है, और यह orange velocity के right angle पर बैठता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇదిగో: force ఆకుపచ్చగా ఉంది, మరియు అది orange velocity కు right angle లో ఉంటుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: And it stays perpendicular the whole way round — always pointing to the centre.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और यह पूरे चक्कर में perpendicular रहता है — हमेशा centre की तरफ point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మరియు ఇది మొత్తం చుట్టూ perpendicular గా ఉంటుంది — ఎప్పుడూ centre వైపు point చేస్తుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: You found this exact direction with the right-hand rule last lesson.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आपने यही exact direction पिछले lesson में right-hand rule से ढूंढी थी।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ exact direction ని మీరు గత lesson లో right-hand rule తో కనుగొన్నారు।

misconception_watch.belief: The force leans forward to drive the charge along its motion.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The F arrow sits square across v, never along it — it points to the centre, not ahead.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: F is perpendicular to v — it cannot push the charge forward.

STATE_3.title: A Sideways Push Does No Work — W = F·d·cos90° = 0

scene_composition.text: the charge takes a small step d — along the velocity

F is at 90° to d
W = F·d·cos90° = 0

work-energy theorem:
zero work → energy can't change

tts_sentences.text_en: The particle moves a little step — that step d is along the velocity.
tts_sentences.text_hi: particle एक छोटा सा step लेता है — वह step d, velocity की दिशा में है।
tts_sentences.text_te: particle ఒక చిన్న step తీసుకుంటుంది — ఆ step d, velocity దిశలో ఉంటుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: The force is at 90° to that step. So how much work does it do — a lot, a little, or none?
tts_sentences.text_hi: force उस step के 90 degrees पर है। तो यह कितना work करता है — बहुत, थोड़ा, या बिल्कुल नहीं?
tts_sentences.text_te: force ఆ step కు 90 degrees లో ఉంది। కాబట్టి ఇది ఎంత work చేస్తుంది — చాలా, కొంచెం, లేదా అస్సలు లేదా?

tts_sentences.text_en: Work is force times displacement times cos of the angle. The angle is 90°.
tts_sentences.text_hi: work होता है force times displacement times cos of the angle। angle है 90 degrees।
tts_sentences.text_te: work అంటే force times displacement times cos of the angle। angle 90 degrees।

tts_sentences.text_en: Cos 90° is zero — so the work done is exactly zero. A sideways push never does work.
tts_sentences.text_hi: cos 90 degrees zero होता है — इसलिए work done बिल्कुल zero है। एक sideways push कभी work नहीं करता।
tts_sentences.text_te: cos 90 degrees zero అవుతుంది — కాబట్టి work done ఖచ్చితంగా zero। ఒక sideways push ఎప్పుడూ work చేయదు।

tts_sentences.text_en: This is the work-energy theorem: zero work means the energy can't change.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही है work-energy theorem: zero work का मतलब है energy नहीं बदल सकती।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇదే work-energy theorem: zero work అంటే energy మారదు।

misconception_watch.belief: Circular motion means work is being done to keep it going.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: F and the displacement d sit at a right angle the whole way round; W = F·d·cos90° = 0.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: A perpendicular force does zero work, always.

STATE_4.title: Speed Locked — the needle never moves

scene_composition.text: Predict: will the speed meter's needle move at all?

|v| meter holds FLAT
for a full orbit
six equal v-arrows → |v| same at every point → ΔKE = 0

tts_sentences.text_en: Zero work means zero change in kinetic energy. Predict the speed meter — will the needle move?
tts_sentences.text_hi: zero work का मतलब है kinetic energy में zero change। speed meter predict करो — क्या needle हिलेगी?
tts_sentences.text_te: zero work అంటే kinetic energy లో zero change। speed meter predict చేయండి — needle కదులుతుందా?

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch it for a full orbit... the needle stays flat. Dead flat.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसे एक पूरे orbit के लिए देखो... needle flat रहती है। बिल्कुल flat।
tts_sentences.text_te: దీన్ని ఒక పూర్తి orbit పాటు చూడండి... needle flat గా ఉంటుంది। పూర్తిగా flat।

tts_sentences.text_en: Now watch — at six points round the circle, I drop a velocity arrow. Every single one is the same length.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब देखो — circle के छह points पर मैं एक velocity arrow डालता हूँ। हर एक की length same है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు చూడండి — circle చుట్టూ ఆరు points వద్ద నేను ఒక velocity arrow వేస్తాను। ప్రతి ఒక్కటి same length ఉంటుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: Same arrow everywhere means the same speed everywhere. It was never speeding up — the speed is locked, only the direction turns.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर जगह same arrow का मतलब है हर जगह same speed। यह कभी speed up नहीं हो रहा था — speed locked है, सिर्फ direction बदलता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అన్ని చోట్లా same arrow అంటే అన్ని చోట్లా same speed। ఇది ఎప్పుడూ speed up అవ్వలేదు — speed locked అయి ఉంది, direction మాత్రమే మారుతుంది।

misconception_watch.belief: Going round and round, the charge must be gaining speed.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The equal-arc trail stays evenly spaced and the |v| meter holds dead flat for a full orbit.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: F ⊥ v → zero work → kinetic energy can't change → the speed is locked.

STATE_5.title: Steers vs Speeds Up — electric first, then magnetic

scene_composition.text:
FIRST — electric:
parabola, speed meter
RISING, work CLIMBING

THEN — magnetic:
curve, speed meter FLAT,
work meter at ZERO
both start at the SAME speed
a magnetic force STEERS — it never speeds you up

tts_sentences.text_en: First, just an electric field. The force here is not sideways — it points along the motion.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पहले, सिर्फ एक electric field। यहाँ force sideways नहीं है — यह motion की direction में point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మొదట, కేవలం ఒక electric field। ఇక్కడ force sideways కాదు — అది motion దిశలో point చేస్తుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the speed meter climb. The electric force does work, so it speeds the charge up.
tts_sentences.text_hi: speed meter को climb करते देखो। electric force work करता है, इसलिए यह charge को speed up करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: speed meter climb అవ్వడం చూడండి। electric force work చేస్తుంది, కాబట్టి అది charge ను speed up చేస్తుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the same charge, same starting speed, but a magnetic field. Predict — will this speed meter climb too?
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब वही charge, same starting speed, लेकिन magnetic field। predict करो — क्या यह speed meter भी climb करेगा?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు అదే charge, same starting speed, కానీ magnetic field। predict చేయండి — ఈ speed meter కూడా climb అవుతుందా?

tts_sentences.text_en: The path curves... but the speed meter stays flat, and the work meter is pinned at zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: path curve होता है... लेकिन speed meter flat रहता है, और work meter zero पर pinned है।
tts_sentences.text_te: path curve అవుతుంది... కానీ speed meter flat గా ఉంటుంది, మరియు work meter zero వద్ద pinned అయి ఉంటుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: That's the whole idea: a magnetic force steers — it never speeds you up.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही पूरा idea है: magnetic force steer करता है — यह कभी speed up नहीं करता।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇదే మొత్తం idea: magnetic force steer చేస్తుంది — అది ఎప్పుడూ speed up చేయదు।

misconception_watch.belief: All forces speed a charge up.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Left (electric) speeds up, right (magnetic) doesn't — same start speed for both.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Only a force with a component along v does work; the magnetic force has none.

STATE_6.title: Your Turn — tighter turn, same speed

scene_composition.text: two sliders: the speed |v|, and the field strength B

bigger B → TIGHTER turn
— but speed meter stays FLAT
stronger field steers harder — it still never adds speed (W = 0)

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Two sliders: the speed, and the magnetic field strength.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी बारी। दो sliders: speed, और magnetic field strength।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ వంతు। రెండు sliders: speed, మరియు magnetic field strength।

tts_sentences.text_en: Crank up magnetic field B and predict — does the particle go faster, or just turn tighter?
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnetic field B बढ़ाओ और predict करो — क्या particle faster जाएगा, या बस tighter turn लेगा?
tts_sentences.text_te: magnetic field B పెంచండి మరియు predict చేయండి — particle faster వెళ్తుందా, లేదా tighter turn తీసుకుంటుందా?

tts_sentences.text_en: Bigger magnetic field B makes a tighter turn — but watch the speed meter: still flat, work still zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बड़ा magnetic field B एक tighter turn बनाता है — लेकिन speed meter देखो: अभी भी flat, work अभी भी zero।
tts_sentences.text_te: పెద్ద magnetic field B ఒక tighter turn చేస్తుంది — కానీ speed meter చూడండి: ఇంకా flat, work ఇంకా zero।

tts_sentences.text_en: Stronger field steers harder. It still never adds speed.
tts_sentences.text_hi: stronger field ज़्यादा hard steer करता है। यह अभी भी कभी speed नहीं जोड़ता।
tts_sentences.text_te: stronger field మరింత కష్టంగా steer చేస్తుంది। అది ఇంకా ఎప్పుడూ speed జోడించదు।

misconception_watch.belief: Turn up B and the charge speeds up.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Raising the B slider tightens the turn while the |v| meter stays flat.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: More B = a TIGHTER turn at the SAME speed — never faster.

STATE_1.label: Round and round — so it must be speeding up?

tts_sentences.text_en: A charged particle drifts in, and we switch on a magnetic field.
Watch — its path curls round and round into a closed loop.
It's going round and round... so it must be speeding up — right?

STATE_2.label: Square across — F is perpendicular to v

tts_sentences.text_en: Before I show the force — which way does the magnetic force point as it orbits?
Here it is: the force is green, and it sits at a right angle to the orange velocity.
And it stays perpendicular the whole way round — always pointing to the centre.
You found this exact direction with the right-hand rule last lesson.

STATE_3.label: A sideways push does no work — W = F·d·cos90° = 0

tts_sentences.text_en: The particle moves a little step — that step d is along the velocity.
The force is at 90° to that step. So how much work does it do — a lot, a little, or none?
Work is force times displacement times cos of the angle. The angle is 90°.
Cos 90° is zero — so the work done is exactly zero. A sideways push never does work.
This is the work-energy theorem: zero work means the energy can't change.

STATE_4.label: Speed locked — the needle never moves

tts_sentences.text_en: Zero work means zero change in kinetic energy. Predict the speed meter — will the needle move?
Watch it for a full orbit... the needle stays flat. Dead flat.
Now watch — at six points round the circle, I drop a velocity arrow. Every single one is the same length.
Same arrow everywhere means the same speed everywhere. It was never speeding up — the speed is locked, only the direction turns.

STATE_5.label: Steers vs speeds up — electric first, then magnetic

tts_sentences.text_en: First, just an electric field. The force here is not sideways — it points along the motion.
Watch the speed meter climb. The electric force does work, so it speeds the charge up.
Now the same charge, same starting speed, but a magnetic field. Predict — will this speed meter climb too?
The path curves... but the speed meter stays flat, and the work meter is pinned at zero.
That's the whole idea: a magnetic force steers — it never speeds you up.

STATE_6.label: Your turn — tighter turn, same speed

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Two sliders: the speed, and the magnetic field strength.
Crank up B and predict — does the particle go faster, or just turn tighter?
Bigger B makes a tighter turn — but watch the speed meter: still flat, work still zero.
Stronger field steers harder. It still never adds speed.
